items.headline: Wild will be without Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin for first two games of series vs. Avs
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — The Minnesota Wild will be without forward Joel Eriksson Ek and defenseman Jonas Brodin for the first two games of their second-round series against the Colorado Avalanche because of lower-body injuries.
Neither player made the trip to Denver. Wild coach John Hynes said the team is still “making a couple decisions on the lineup" ahead of Game 1 on Sunday night.

items.headline: AC Milan slip at Sassuolo, Juventus stumble against Verona
items.description: Juventus missed a chance to exploit AC Milan's earlier slip and take third place in Serie A as they drew 1-1 at home...

items.headline: Holder shines as Gujarat beat Punjab in nervy IPL win
items.description: Jason holder stood out with figures of 4-24 in an inspired pace attack to set up a four-wicket win for Gujarat Titans in a thrilling IPL...

items.headline: Braves place Ronald Acuña Jr. on the 10-day injured list with a strained hamstring
items.description:
DENVER (AP) — The Atlanta Braves placed star right fielder Ronald Acuña Jr. on the 10-day injured list because of a strained left hamstring on Sunday.
The Braves owned the best record in baseball at 24-10 entering Sunday's game against the Colorado Rockies. But they'll have to get by for now without one of their top players.

items.headline: Man Utd beat Liverpool to secure Champions League place

items.headline: Cubs activate closer Daniel Palencia from the 15-day injured list
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Cubs closer Daniel Palencia was activated from the 15-day injured list on Sunday after being sidelined by a left oblique strain.
Palencia pitched five scoreless innings over five appearances before he was placed on the IL on April 17. The right-hander made one rehab appearance, reaching 102 mph while recording two outs for Triple-A Iowa on Friday.
